changelog: v19: Keyless desktop Focus question generation. research_expand gains `--from-response <file>`: paired with the existing `--prompt`, the agent (Claude Code) reads the emitted prompt, writes the questions JSON, and the script deposits candidates + neighborhood with no model call. The /focus skill now documents this desktop path (emit prompt → agent writes questions → deposit → promote), so adding a Focus needs no API key or gateway on the desktop; cron still uses OpenClaw/Anthropic. (serve has no model call and was already keyless.) v18: Keyless desktop wiki synthesis. `compile --emit-tasks <path>` writes per-page synthesis tasks (sources + related candidates) with no model call; the agent (Claude Code) writes each page's prose to state/synthesis/<slug>.md, and the next `compile` consumes those drafts into the cache and assembles the graph — so /compile runs on the desktop with no API key or gateway. Synthesis source order per page: cache → agent draft → OpenClaw/Anthropic (cron) → excerpt fallback. Related links are taken from an optional `Related:` line or inferred from [[wikilinks]] in the prose. v17: Wiki becomes a navigable graph. wiki_compile rewritten as plan→synthesize→cross-link→write: pages are now LLM-synthesized prose with content-derived related links (OpenClaw-first, Anthropic fallback, deterministic excerpt fallback when no LLM), plus auto-derived backlinks and shared-source 'see also' edges. Syntheses are cached in state/wiki_synthesis_cache.json so re-compiles are idempotent and cheap (--no-ai forces offline mode; new wiki_model config key). serve_wiki now resolves [[wikilinks]] to real pages (slug→page index with type-priority collision handling) instead of always bouncing to search. New `/compile` Claude Code/Desktop skill drives `lifehug.py compile` (now with --no-ai/--model passthrough). v16.1: Answer quality loop — implicit richness scoring accumulates per answer; weekly profile aggregation feeds multipliers into planner weights and AI expansion prompts; zero friction. v16: Guided Focus manager. One-shot `lifehug.py focus-new` scaffolds a new question-bank category, registers the Focus, and auto-generates+promotes ~8-12 starter questions (graceful scaffold-only fallback when no AI is available). New `/focus` Claude Code skill and OpenClaw message protocol walk through add/show/finish/tune. Adds category scaffolding (roadmap.scaffold_category) and bulk candidate promotion (candidates promote-neighborhood). Includes v15.1: OpenClaw-first AI routing in research_expand (keyless generation when OpenClaw is running; Anthropic SDK fallback). v15: Focus model + dynamic Focus-weighted allocation + self-knowledge/relational neighborhoods + wiki relationships/self surfaces + progress dashboard + three-tier scheduling.
